name: Housing Foundation of America - Lake Worth Florida
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit providing free homebuyer counseling, financial literacy workshops, and down payment assistance programs across Florida and North Carolina.
description_long:
Housing Foundation of America, Inc. is a HUD-approved 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that has operated for 10 years, establishing itself as a leading provider of housing-related financial counseling and homebuyer assistance. The organization focuses on removing barriers to homeownership through education, counseling, and direct financial aid for qualified borrowers. Their service area spans multiple counties in Florida (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, St. Lucie, Martin, Brevard, Lee) and North Carolina (Guilford, Mecklenburg, Forsyth, Wake, Orange), with virtual and in-person options. They are certified by HUD and structured as a legitimate nonprofit, qualifying them to provide unbiased counseling without profit motives.

HFoA offers comprehensive homebuyer services including pre-purchase and post-purchase counseling, credit and budgeting education, loss mitigation assistance, mortgage delinquency resolution, home inspection tools, and foreclosure prevention guidance. They conduct HUD-certified 8-hour homebuyer education courses, operate virtual Homebuyer Clubs, provide access to down payment assistance programs (documented examples include $74,000 from Broward County and $10,000 from Bank of America), and maintain partnerships with lenders, realtors, and builders. Their counseling addresses credit rebuilding, money management, debt management, and home repair rehabilitation.

The organization distinguishes itself through HUD certification, which ensures compliance with federal standards for housing counseling, and their documented track record of connecting clients to substantial down payment assistance funds. They offer multiple service delivery formats (in-person, virtual, online through eHome America platform) and maintain an active partnership network. Their Minority Builders Coalition and new construction property listings indicate commitment to addressing equity in homeownership access. The website includes dedicated foreclosure prevention resources and scam avoidance education, suggesting comprehensive consumer protection focus.

Limitations include a website marked as "not activated," which may indicate outdated or incomplete online presence, potentially making current program information and registration difficult to access. Service availability appears concentrated in specific geographic regions, limiting accessibility for those outside their coverage areas. The website lacks detailed current program eligibility requirements, pricing structures, and timeline information, requiring direct contact for specifics. The organization's status as free/low-cost should be verified directly, as some HUD-approved counselors charge nominal fees.
pros: HUD-certified and 501(c)(3) nonprofit status ensures unbiased, federally-compliant counseling without profit incentives
Documented success connecting clients to substantial down payment assistance ($74,000+ awards mentioned)
Comprehensive service menu addressing full homebuyer lifecycle: pre-purchase, purchase, post-purchase, and foreclosure prevention
Multiple service delivery methods including virtual options (eHome America online platform, Virtual H2H clubs)
Partnership network with lenders, realtors, and builders providing integrated pathway to homeownership
Credit and financial literacy focus with dedicated courses on credit rebuilding, budgeting, and savings basics
Specific foreclosure prevention and scam avoidance resources for at-risk borrowers
cons: Website marked 'not activated' with navigation appearing duplicated/broken, suggesting outdated online presence and difficulty accessing current information
Geographic service limitations restricted to specific Florida counties (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, St. Lucie, Martin, Brevard, Lee) and North Carolina counties only
Limited transparency on website regarding current program eligibility requirements, fees (if any), wait times, or enrollment processes
Phone contact required (954-923-5001) to access current program details, reducing convenience for initial information gathering
No clear timeline or application status tracking system visible on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Florida/North Carolina service areas needing credit education and down payment assistance
Borrowers facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency seeking loss mitigation counseling and default resolution assistance
Self-employed or credit-challenged individuals needing comprehensive financial and housing counseling before applying for mortgages
Homeowners seeking post-purchase counseling, home repair guidance, or refinancing counseling
diagnosis: Housing Foundation of America is best for first-time homebuyers and at-risk borrowers in their service areas (specific Florida and North Carolina counties) who need free or low-cost HUD-certified counseling, financial literacy education, and guidance accessing down payment assistance programs. The primary caveat is that their website's non-activated status creates friction for initial research—prospective clients must contact them directly at 954-923-5001 or visit in person to confirm current program availability, eligibility, and enrollment procedures.
services: Credit essentials and credit rebuilding counseling
Budgeting and money management/debt management education
Mortgage delinquency and default resolution assistance
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Down payment assistance program navigation and connection
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 8461 Lake Worth Rd, Lake Worth, FL 33467
